prompt: A rigged 3D model of a hawk moth (Nocturnal Sentinel). 6 legs, two large wings, feathery antennae. Fully rigged for animation with bones for wings and legs. Steampunk insect style matching the reference image. The model should face +Z. It is a flying insect. High detail, clean geometry, production ready. Individual parts should be correctly parented to bones. Correct UV layout for steampunk materials. Rig should be 'Generic' for Unity. The model's head should face +Z. Wing bones are essential for flight animations. Legs are also rigged. High-quality PBR material. Mesh faces should be oriented correctly. No stray vertices. All face normals should be consistent. The resulting prefab should be ready for animation. The thorax is the root of the rig. Body parts are distinct but unified into a single skeletal hierarchy. The style is inspired by industrial steampunk with brass, copper, and clockwork details. The wings have intricate gears and clockwork patterns as part of the texture. The antennae are feathery and elegant. The 6 legs are mechanical in appearance. This is a support healer unit. It has an ethereal and delicate presence. No combat or offensive features. High-fidelity skeletal mesh. Correct pivot at the center of the thorax. This is intended to be used with the Unity Generic Rig for skeletal animations. Final asset is a Unity Prefab. Wings should have at least 3 bones each for complex deformation. 6 legs should have 3 segments each. Body consists of Head, Thorax, and Abdomen. Head should have bone for movement. Thorax is the main hub. Abdomen has its own bone for breathing/movement simulation. Animation is the goal for this rig. The rig must be stable and consistent. All bone names should be logical and clean. Forward is +Z. Up is +Y. Mesh density should be appropriate for a production game asset. The visual style matches the 'Nocturnal Sentinel' concept. It's a support unit, so it should look graceful and non-threatening. Its healing aura is a separate visual effect, not part of this model. The model's anatomy should be functional and logical for flight. Large wings are the main feature. Antennae are for sensing and aesthetics. Legs are for landing and walking. Full skeletal hierarchy from Thorax down to all appendages. No hidden or disconnected parts. All geometry is skinned to the rig correctly. Weights should be balanced. Standard PBR material provided with the prefab. The prefab is the final output. Save to Assets/_InsectWars/Units/Hawk Moth/HawkMoth_Rigged.prefab. High resolution textures. All required PBR maps included in the prefab's material. The resulting mesh is clean and has no geometry errors like holes or flipped faces. Unity ready.
